title: Erigeron beyrichii
intro: Erigeron beyrichii, also known as Mexican daisy or Fleabane, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are beneficial for treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron beyrichii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat various health conditions, and its leaves and flowers are also used in folk remedies for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
